anti_patterns.name: non_null_assertion
anti_patterns.description: Non-null assertion (!) bypasses TypeScript's null safety. Use proper null checks or optional chaining instead.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect ! used as non-null assertion operator on expressions.
anti_patterns.fix: Use optional chaining (?.), nullish coalescing (??), or explicit null checks.

anti_patterns.name: console_log_in_production
anti_patterns.description: console.log statements should not be in production code. Use a proper logging library with levels.
anti_patterns.fix: Use a logging library (winston, pino) with configurable log levels. Remove debug console.log calls.

anti_patterns.name: callback_hell
anti_patterns.description: Deeply nested callbacks make code hard to read and maintain. Use async/await or Promise chains.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect 3+ levels of nested callback functions.
anti_patterns.fix: Refactor to async/await. Extract nested callbacks into named functions.

anti_patterns.name: enum_antipattern
anti_patterns.description: TypeScript enums have surprising runtime behavior. Prefer const objects with 'as const' or union types.
anti_patterns.fix: Replace enum with: const STATUS = { Active: 'active', Inactive: 'inactive' } as const; type Status = typeof STATUS[keyof typeof STATUS];

anti_patterns.name: index_signature_overuse
anti_patterns.description: Index signatures ([key: string]: any) lose type safety. Use Map<K,V>, Record<K,V>, or explicit interfaces.
anti_patterns.fix: Use Record<string, SpecificType>, Map<string, SpecificType>, or define proper interface properties.

anti_patterns.name: promise_constructor_antipattern
anti_patterns.description: Wrapping an already-async operation in new Promise() is redundant and error-prone. The inner async function already returns a Promise.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect new Promise() wrapping await or .then() chains.
anti_patterns.fix: Return the async result directly. async functions already return Promises.

anti_patterns.name: typeof_guard_misuse
anti_patterns.description: typeof x === 'object' returns true for null. Always pair with a null check.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect typeof x === 'object' without accompanying null check.
anti_patterns.fix: Use typeof x === 'object' && x !== null, or use a proper type guard function.

anti_patterns.name: non_null_assertion
anti_patterns.description: The ! non-null assertion operator tells TypeScript to trust you, bypassing type safety. If you're wrong, you get a runtime error.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect x!. and x![ non-null assertion usage.
anti_patterns.fix: Use proper null checks: if (x) { x.prop } or optional chaining: x?.prop.
